summary: Standard onboarding flow for new architecture modules, function paradigms, service surfaces, evidence lanes, and contract families.

evidence_rules.minimum_required_cell_evidence: At least one runnable test command or one contract evidence bundle.
evidence_rules.service_surface_rule: A service surface belongs inside an existing module and must not claim owned paths.
evidence_rules.path_rule: All source, evidence, and docs paths must be repository-relative.
evidence_rules.file_size_rule: Source and docs touched by extension work should stay under the project file-line guard.

steps: Declare the module id, layer, owned paths, dependencies, benchmark lanes, security lanes, and risk tags in module-topology.
Add required function paradigms and cell statuses in module-function-coverage-matrix.
Ensure the coverage tensor reports no required_gap, missing, or weak_evidence cells.
Document ownership, non-ownership, and handoff boundaries.
Add the paradigm description to the matrix.
Map the paradigm to benchmark and security lanes in the tensor.
Update required_by_module only for modules that truly own that capability.
Run matrix and tensor checks before claiming coverage.
Attach the service surface to its owning module rather than creating a fake module.
Bind runtime API families or readiness reports when the surface exposes shared contracts.
Document whether the surface is product shell, control API, self-host web, or runtime-facing.
Declare the benchmark or security lane centrally.
Attach at least one command to lane_test_plan.
Map the lane from relevant paradigms in the tensor.
Verify the lane creates evidence_depth for required cells.
Add repository-relative source files and client surfaces.
Add schema or text anchors to the appropriate release contract.
Attach contract evidence to the tensor when the family proves a required paradigm.
Run the family checker and the full architecture gate.
